title: Crispy Peruvian Tofu Bowl
description: A savory, crispy tofu bowl with quinoa, poached frozen berries, and Peruvian aji amarillo seasoning, perfect for a sick day and halal-friendly.
instructions: Press the tofu to remove excess moisture by wrapping in paper towels and placing a heavy object on top for 10 minutes.
In a bowl, combine the tofu cubes with soy sauce, aji amarillo paste, ground cumin, garlic powder, salt, and pepper; let marinate for 5 minutes.
Dredge the marinated tofu in cornstarch until lightly coated.
Heat 1 tbsp of olive oil in a skillet over medium‑high heat and fry the tofu until golden and crispy, about 4‑5 minutes per side. Transfer to paper towels to drain.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp of oil to the skillet, then rinse quinoa and add it with the vegetable broth; bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the liquid is absorbed.
Warm the frozen berries in a small saucepan over low heat until just thawed, about 2‑3 minutes; do not cook further so they retain their texture.
To assemble, spoon the cooked quinoa into each bowl, top with the crispy tofu, then add a generous amount of warmed berries.
Drizzle lime juice over the bowl, sprinkle chopped cilantro, and season with additional salt if desired.
Serve immediately, optionally with extra lime wedges on the side.
